L. 88–365, § 12(c)(10)78 Stat. 306Pub. L. 89–117, § 110979 Stat. 507Pub. L. 89–562, § 2(a)(1)80 Stat. 715Pub. L. 90–19, § 2081 Stat. 25Pub. L. 90–448, § 70282 Stat. 535Pub. L. 100–17, § 318(b)(1)101 Stat. 2345302(a)(13)49 App.:1608(c)(11).5302(b)49 App.:1608(c)(4) (last sentence).In this chapter, the words “local governmental authority” are substituted for “local public body” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.In subsection (a), before clause (1), the text of 49 App.:1608(c)(7) is omitted as surplus. The text of 49 App.:1608(c)(8) is omitted because the complete title of the Secretary of Transportation is used the first time the term appears in a section. In clause (1), before subclause (A), the words “capital project” are substituted for “construction” for clarity. In subclause (A), the words “actual”, “all”, and “reconstruction” are omitted as surplus. In subclause (D), the words “(whether or not such overhaul increases the useful life of the rolling stock)” are omitted as surplus. In clause (2), the words “for each of the jurisdictions included in the definition of ‘State’ ” are omitted as surplus. In clauses (3) and (10), the word “regulation” is substituted for “rule” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the Code and because the terms are synonymous. In clause (3)(B)(iii), the words “of persons” are omitted as surplus. In clauses (4) and (5), the word “mass” is substituted for “public” because of the restatement. In clause (4)(A), the words “including, but not limited to, fixed rail, automated guideway transit, and exclusive facilities for buses” are omitted as surplus. In clause (6)(A), the words “municipalities and other” are omitted as surplus. In clause (6)(B), the word “authority” is substituted for “public agencies and instrumentalities” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the Code. The word “municipalities” is omitted as surplus. In clause (7), the words “bus, or rail, or other”, “either publicly or privately owned”, and “on a . . . basis” are omitted as surplus. Clause (8) is added for clarity because the term “net project cost” has the same meaning throughout this chapter. In clause (11), the words “the Commonwealths of” are omitted as surplus. In clause (12), the word “individuals” is substituted for “commuters or others” to eliminate unnecessary words. In clause (13)(A), the words “in the case of any such area” and “entire” are omitted as surplus. The words “Secretary of Commerce” are substituted for “Bureau of the Census” because of 15:1511(e). In clause (13)(B), the words “so designated by the Bureau of Census”, “which shall be”, “responsible”, and “in cooperation with each other” are omitted as surplus.Subsection (b) applies to section 5307(d)(1)(D) of the revised title because of 49 App.:1607a(e)(1), restated as section 5307(n)(2) of the revised title.

L. 100–17, § 318(b)(1)101 Stat. 2345302(a)(13)49 App.:1608(c)(11).5302(b)49 App.:1608(c)(4) (last sentence).In this chapter, the words “local governmental authority” are substituted for “local public body” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.In subsection (a), before clause (1), the text of 49 App.:1608(c)(7) is omitted as surplus. The text of 49 App.:1608(c)(8) is omitted because the complete title of the Secretary of Transportation is used the first time the term appears in a section. In clause (1), before subclause (A), the words “capital project” are substituted for “construction” for clarity. In subclause (A), the words “actual”, “all”, and “reconstruction” are omitted as surplus. In subclause (D), the words “(whether or not such overhaul increases the useful life of the rolling stock)” are omitted as surplus. In clause (2), the words “for each of the jurisdictions included in the definition of ‘State’ ” are omitted as surplus. In clauses (3) and (10), the word “regulation” is substituted for “rule” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the Code and because the terms are synonymous. In clause (3)(B)(iii), the words “of persons” are omitted as surplus. In clauses (4) and (5), the word “mass” is substituted for “public” because of the restatement. In clause (4)(A), the words “including, but not limited to, fixed rail, automated guideway transit, and exclusive facilities for buses” are omitted as surplus. In clause (6)(A), the words “municipalities and other” are omitted as surplus. In clause (6)(B), the word “authority” is substituted for “public agencies and instrumentalities” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the Code. The word “municipalities” is omitted as surplus. In clause (7), the words “bus, or rail, or other”, “either publicly or privately owned”, and “on a . . . basis” are omitted as surplus. Clause (8) is added for clarity because the term “net project cost” has the same meaning throughout this chapter. In clause (11), the words “the Commonwealths of” are omitted as surplus. In clause (12), the word “individuals” is substituted for “commuters or others” to eliminate unnecessary words. In clause (13)(A), the words “in the case of any such area” and “entire” are omitted as surplus. The words “Secretary of Commerce” are substituted for “Bureau of the Census” because of 15:1511(e). In clause (13)(B), the words “so designated by the Bureau of Census”, “which shall be”, “responsible”, and “in cooperation with each other” are omitted as surplus.Subsection (b) applies to section 5307(d)(1)(D) of the revised title because of 49 App.:1607a(e)(1), restated as section 5307(n)(2) of the revised title.

children.heading: .—Assault on a transit worker
children.label: 1 .—Assault on a transit worker
children.text: The term “assault on a transit worker” means a circumstance in which an individual knowingly, without lawful authority or permission, and with intent to endanger the safety of any individual, or with a reckless disregard for the safety of human life, interferes with, disables, or incapacitates a transit worker while the transit worker is performing the duties of the transit worker.

children.label: A
children.text: historic preservation, rehabilitation, and operation of historic public transportation buildings, structures, and facilities (including historic bus and railroad facilities) intended for use in public transportation service;

children.label: B
children.text: bus shelters;

children.label: C
children.text: functional landscaping and streetscaping, including benches, trash receptacles, and street lights;

children.label: D
children.text: pedestrian access and walkways;

children.label: E
children.text: bicycle access, including bicycle storage shelters and parking facilities and the installation of equipment for transporting bicycles on public transportation vehicles;

children.label: F
children.text: signage; or

children.label: G
children.text: enhanced access for persons with disabilities to public transportation.

children.label: A
children.text: in which the majority of each line operates in a separated right-of-way dedicated for public transportation use during peak periods; and

children.label: i
children.text: defined stations;

children.label: ii
children.text: traffic signal priority for public transportation vehicles;

children.label: iii
children.text: short headway bidirectional services for a substantial part of weekdays and weekend days; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other features the Secretary may determine are necessary to produce high-quality public transportation services that emulate the services provided by rail fixed guideway public transportation systems.

children.label: A
children.text: acquiring, constructing, supervising, or inspecting equipment or a facility for use in public transportation, expenses incidental to the acquisition or construction (including designing, engineering, location surveying, mapping, and acquiring rights-of-way), payments for the capital portions of rail trackage rights agreements, transit-related intelligent transportation systems, relocation assistance, acquiring replacement housing sites, and acquiring, constructing, relocating, and rehabilitating replacement housing;

children.label: B
children.text: rehabilitating a bus;

children.label: C
children.text: remanufacturing a bus;

children.label: D
children.text: overhauling rail rolling stock;

children.label: E
children.text: preventive maintenance;

children.label: F
children.text: leasing equipment or a facility for use in public transportation;

children.label: i
children.text: enhances economic development or incorporates private investment, such as commercial and residential development;

children.label: I
children.text: enhances the effectiveness of public transportation and is related physically or functionally to public transportation; or

children.label: II
children.text: establishes new or enhanced coordination between public transportation and other transportation;

children.label: iii
children.text: provides a fair share of revenue that will be used for public transportation;

children.label: iv
children.text: provides that if equipment to fuel privately owned zero-emission passenger vehicles is installed, the recipient of assistance under this chapter shall collect fees from users of the equipment in order to recover the costs of construction, maintenance, and operation of the equipment;

children.label: v
children.text: provides that a person making an agreement to occupy space in a facility constructed under this paragraph shall pay a fair share of the costs of the facility through rental payments and other means; and

children.label: I
children.text: property acquisition;

children.label: II
children.text: demolition of existing structures;

children.label: III
children.text: site preparation;

children.label: IV
children.text: utilities;

children.label: V
children.text: building foundations;

children.label: VI
children.text: walkways;

children.label: VII
children.text: pedestrian and bicycle access to a public transportation facility;

children.label: VIII
children.text: construction, renovation, and improvement of intercity bus and intercity rail stations and terminals;

children.label: IX
children.text: renovation and improvement of historic transportation facilities;

children.label: X
children.text: open space;

children.label: XI
children.text: safety and security equipment and facilities (including lighting, surveillance, and related intelligent transportation system applications);

children.label: XII
children.text: facilities that incorporate community services such as daycare or health care;

children.label: XIII
children.text: a capital project for, and improving, equipment or a facility for an intermodal transfer facility or transportation mall;

children.label: XIV
children.text: construction of space for commercial uses; and

children.label: XV
children.text: technology to fuel a zero-emission vehicle;

children.label: H
children.text: the introduction of new technology, through innovative and improved products, into public transportation;

children.label: i
children.text: not to exceed 10 percent of such recipient’s annual formula apportionment under sections 5307 and 5311; or

children.label: I
children.text: Provides an active fixed route travel training program that is available for riders with disabilities.

children.label: II
children.text: Provides that all fixed route and paratransit operators participate in a passenger safety, disability awareness, and sensitivity training class on at least a biennial basis.

children.label: III
children.text: Has memoranda of understanding in place with employers and the American Job Center to increase access to employment opportunities for people with disabilities.

children.label: J
children.text: establishing a debt service reserve, made up of deposits with a bondholder’s trustee, to ensure the timely payment of principal and interest on bonds issued by a grant recipient to finance an eligible project under this chapter;

children.label: i
children.text: consisting of short-range planning and management activities and projects for improving coordination among public transportation and other transportation service providers carried out by a recipient or subrecipient through an agreement entered into with a person, including a governmental entity, under this chapter (other than section 5309); but

children.label: ii
children.text: excluding operating public transportation services;

children.label: i
children.text: equipment, tires, tubes, and material, each costing at least .5 percent of the current fair market value of rolling stock comparable to the rolling stock for which the equipment, tires, tubes, and material are to be used; and

children.label: ii
children.text: reconstruction of equipment and material, each of which after reconstruction will have a fair market value of at least .5 percent of the current fair market value of rolling stock comparable to the rolling stock for which the equipment and material will be used;

children.label: M
children.text: associated transit improvements; or

children.label: N
children.text: technological changes or innovations to modify low or no emission vehicles (as defined in section 5339(c)) or facilities.

children.label: A
children.text: an entity designated, in accordance with the planning process under sections 5303 and 5304, by the Governor of a State, responsible local officials, and publicly owned operators of public transportation, to receive and apportion amounts under section 5336 to urbanized areas of 200,000 or more in population; or

children.label: B
children.text: a State or regional authority, if the authority is responsible under the laws of a State for a capital project and for financing and directly providing public transportation.

children.heading: .—Disability
children.label: 6 .—Disability
children.text: The term “disability” has the same meaning as in section 3(1) of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ().42 U.S.C. 12102

children.label: A
children.text: that is effective temporarily before the expiration of the otherwise specified periods of time for public notice and comment under section 5334(c); and

children.label: i
children.text: would injure seriously an important public interest;

children.label: ii
children.text: would frustrate substantially legislative policy and intent; or

children.label: iii
children.text: would damage seriously a person or class without serving an important public interest.

children.label: A
children.text: using and occupying a separate right-of-way for the exclusive use of public transportation;

children.label: B
children.text: using rail;

children.label: C
children.text: using a fixed catenary system;

children.label: D
children.text: for a passenger ferry system; or

children.label: E
children.text: for a bus rapid transit system.

children.label: A
children.text: means the Governor of a State, the mayor of the District of Columbia, and the chief executive officer of a territory of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes the designee of the Governor.

children.heading: Job access and reverse commute project.—
children.label: 10 Job access and reverse commute project.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The term “job access and reverse commute project” means a transportation project to finance planning, capital, and operating costs that support the development and maintenance of transportation services designed to transport welfare recipients and eligible low-income individuals to and from jobs and activities related to their employment, including transportation projects that facilitate the provision of public transportation services from urbanized areas and rural areas to suburban employment locations.

children.heading: .—Eligible low-income individual
children.label: i .—Eligible low-income individual
children.text: The term “eligible low-income individual” means an individual whose family income is at or below 150 percent of the poverty line (as that term is defined in section 673(2) of the Community Service Block Grant Act (), including any revision required by that section) for a family of the size involved.42 U.S.C. 9902(2)

children.heading: .—Welfare recipient
children.label: ii .—Welfare recipient
children.text: The term “welfare recipient” means an individual who has received assistance under a State or tribal program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) at any time during the 3-year period before the date on which the applicant applies for a grant under section 5307 or 5311.42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: A
children.text: a political subdivision of a State;

children.label: B
children.text: an authority of at least 1 State or political subdivision of a State;

children.label: C
children.text: an Indian tribe; and

children.label: D
children.text: a public corporation, board, or commission established under the laws of a State.

children.heading: .—Low-income individual
children.label: 12 .—Low-income individual
children.text: The term “low-income individual” means an individual whose family income is at or below 150 percent of the poverty line, as that term is defined in section 673(2) of the Community Services Block Grant Act (), including any revision required by that section, for a family of the size involved.42 U.S.C. 9902(2)

children.heading: .—Net project cost
children.label: 13 .—Net project cost
children.text: The term “net project cost” means the part of a project that reasonably cannot be financed from revenues.

children.label: A
children.text: that has not been used in public transportation in the United States before the date of production of the model; or

children.label: B
children.text: used in public transportation in the United States, but being produced with a major change in configuration or components.

children.label: A
children.text: means regular, continuing shared-ride surface transportation services that are open to the general public or open to a segment of the general public defined by age, disability, or low income; and

children.label: i
children.text: intercity passenger rail transportation provided by the entity described in chapter 243 (or a successor to such entity);

children.label: ii
children.text: intercity bus service;

children.label: iii
children.text: charter bus service;

children.label: iv
children.text: school bus service;

children.label: v
children.text: sightseeing service;

children.label: vi
children.text: courtesy shuttle service for patrons of one or more specific establishments; or

children.label: vii
children.text: intra-terminal or intra-facility shuttle services.

children.heading: .—Regulation
children.label: 16 .—Regulation
children.text: The term “regulation” means any part of a statement of general or particular applicability of the Secretary designed to carry out, interpret, or prescribe law or policy in carrying out this chapter.

children.heading: .—Rural area
children.label: 17 .—Rural area
children.text: The term “rural area” means an area encompassing a population of less than 50,000 people that has not been designated in the most recent decennial census as an “urbanized area” by the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: .—Secretary
children.label: 18 .—Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Transportation.

children.heading: .—Senior
children.label: 19 .—Senior
children.text: The term “senior” means an individual who is 65 years of age or older.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 20 .—State
children.text: The term “State” means a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Virgin Islands.

children.heading: .—State of good repair
children.label: 21 .—State of good repair
children.text: The term “state of good repair” has the meaning given that term by the Secretary, by rule, under section 5326(b).

children.heading: .—Transit
children.label: 22 .—Transit
children.text: The term “transit” means public transportation.

children.heading: .—Urban area
children.label: 23 .—Urban area
children.text: The term “urban area” means an area that includes a municipality or other built-up place that the Secretary, after considering local patterns and trends of urban growth, decides is appropriate for a local public transportation system to serve individuals in the locality.

children.heading: .—Urbanized area
children.label: 24 .—Urbanized area
children.text: The term “urbanized area” means an area encompassing a population of not less than 50,000 people that has been defined and designated in the most recent decennial census as an “urbanized area” by the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: .—Value capture
children.label: 25 .—Value capture
children.text: The term “value capture” means recovering the increased property value to property located near public transportation resulting from investments in public transportation.
